rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BEATRICE FORSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/24/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Schedule B still shows the carry values of the US Treasury Bills that are not the same as the carry values at the beginning of the accounting period. (2) Is the conservator affiliated with the proposed trustee? (3) The Joinder Agreement is blank, although paragraph (b) of Section 6.04 of the Master Trust provides for the distribution of remaining assets "in accordance with the terms of the Joinder Agreement." (4) The Master Trust complies with CRC 7.903 by requiring the trustee to account and have fees approved by the court. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/10/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT PON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/23/2025. Issue necessitating continuance: 1) Notice is insufficient. The supplemental declaration filed 10/31/2025 requests relief materially different than the relief requested in the underlying petition, and is considered an amendment. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends (CRC 7.53). Please address the above issue and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 12/16/2025 to avoid further continuance.

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the petitioner never set forth the identity or date of death of the decedent's mother, as required by LRSF 14.18 C. and 14.18 D. (Examiner was able to track down the information in a related case filed in 2008.) Status of closing the estate will be set for 5/18/27. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 5/18/27. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PEGGY LYNCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authorization To Return Assets To Trust, Including Conservatees Interest In Apn 249-48-012 Commonly Known As 235 E. Julian In San Jose, Ca; To Effectuate Any And All Documents As Needed
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been cleared/addressed: (1) Further allegations are needed regarding the petitioner's standing under Probate Code Section 850(a)(3). It must be clarified whether the petitioner is acting as a trustee or as an interested person. If the latter, please provide additional details demonstrating how the petitioner qualifies as an interested person. (2) Need allegations about whether the property is currently in someone else's possession (such as a tenant or renter). If it is, the individuals in possession must be served notice in accordance with Civil Code Procedure Section 413.10, as required by Probate Code Section 851(a)(2). (3) Need information about who the successor-in-interest is for John's estate, including whether he died testate. If he did die testate, a copy of his will must be provided. (4) Need clarification that this petition is for the 50% being titled as community property by John and Peggy in the real property 233 and 235 E. Julian, or only 235 E. Julian. Deeds and Schedule A present 235 E only; however, the petition sets forth 233 and 235 E. Julian. (5) Need an authority under which the court should allow the petitioner, in which capacity, to sign the affidavit for the purpose of transferring John's shares to Peggy. (6) Proposed order must be provided. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TINA MEI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance will be required as the following issues have not been cleared or addressed: (1) Disclaimer is irrevocable and not an assignment, disposing of the disclaimed interests as if the disclaimant predeceased the decedent. Prob. Code sections. 281. Petitioner alleges that Ms. A. Mei has two children who are entitled to her share as a result of the disclaimer. It appears that the petition needs to be properly amended to mirror the said proposed distribution (to Ms. A. Mei's issues, considering one issue is a minor), or the petitioner needs to provide an authority under which the court can grant the proposed distribution of Ms. A. Mei's shares to the petitioner, not her children. (2) The date of birth of the minor child of Ms. A. Mei. (3) The children of the disclaimant must be properly noticed. (4) Assets included in the Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) must be identified to ensure that all accounts are accurately listed on Attachment 1. If any account contains securities held in a brokerage account, these must be appraised by the probate referee. Listing merely the names of the financial institutions is insufficient. In such cases, a new probate referee must be appointed, and a corrected Inventory and Appraisal must be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LYNCH FAMILY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Forconfirmation Of Validity Of Estate Plan Documents; Account And Return Of Trust Assets; Double Damages; And Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The parties are to report to the Court on the status of the case. The following issues have not been cleared: (1) Notice of Hearing not on file. (2) Petition to set forth under which capacity the petitioner is asking for the respondents' account. Paragraph 48 alleges that he is a 6% rental income beneficiary. If so, the petition needs to set forth the information on whether the trustee has failed to submit a requested account within 60 days after a written request. Prob. Code 17200(b(7).(C). (3) Proposed order not submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD A. GARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/23/2025. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) The original will does not appear to have been lodged with the court (Probate Code Section 8200(a)(1)). 2) No proof of publication has been filed (Probate Code Sections 8120 and 8121). 3) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends (CRC 7.53). Please address the above concerns and provide courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 12/16/2025 to avoid further continuance.
